title: WorkOrderType
description: A configurable classification of work an organisation performs (e.g. Preventative, Reactive, Administrative, Operations). Turns the work-order 'type' from a fixed enum into a seedable, extensible catalogue. A WorkOrder references one of these by its code. One JSON document = one work-order type. Plantree ships a built-in set out of the box; organisations may add, re-label, deactivate or reorder types, but the four built-in codes (PM, RM, AD, OP) cannot be deleted — only deactivated — so that history and reporting stay stable.

code.description: Human-facing unique code an org standardises on and reports by, e.g. PM, RM, AD, OP. Uppercase, 2-6 chars. Referenced by WorkOrder.workOrderTypeCode; immutable once work exists against it.

category.description: Top-level grouping for rollups and cost analysis. 'maintenance' is work against an asset's health (PM/RM); 'operations' is running/operating work (rounds, adjustments); 'administrative' is non-maintenance overhead (meetings, training, travel).

nature.description: Analytical nature of the work, driving the proactive-vs-reactive maintenance KPI. Preventive work is 'proactive'; breakdown/corrective work is 'reactive'; non-maintenance work is 'not_applicable'.

defaultPriority.description: Priority pre-filled on a new work order of this type, on the org's 1-5 scale (1 = most urgent). Null = no default; the planner sets it.

requiresAsset.description: Whether a work order of this type must name an asset. Maintenance types require one; administrative/operational work often does not.

requiresFailureReport.description: Whether closing a work order of this type expects failure/cause/remedy coding (a FailureReport). True for reactive maintenance so breakdowns feed reliability analysis.

workflowProfile.description: Key of the lifecycle profile (state machine) this type follows. Null = the organisation's default profile (see work-management-lifecycle). Lets, e.g., a low-risk administrative flow skip review/approval while reactive work keeps the full path.

colour.description: Hex colour used to tint this type in boards, calendars and charts.

icon.description: Icon token for UI (implementation-agnostic name, e.g. 'calendar-check').

sortOrder.description: Display order in pickers and legends; ascending. Null sorts after numbered entries, then by code.

system.description: True for the built-in types Plantree seeds. System types may be re-labelled, recoloured, reordered or deactivated, but not deleted.

active.description: Whether this type can be chosen for new work orders. Deactivating hides it from pickers without affecting historical work already coded to it.

rev.description: Optimistic-concurrency revision; bumped on every save.
